channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Israelis denounce Trump’s deal with Iran
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Lior Soroka
item.description: Israeli officials disparaged the peace deal and said Israel’s fight against Hezbollah in Lebanon would go on.

item.title: After news of Iran peace deal, Lebanese return home to assess wreckage
item.dc:creator: Mohamad El Chamaa, Suzan Haidamous
item.description: Some war-weary residents displaced from the south found relative quiet, with guarded optimism that Trump will pressure Israel to adhere to a truce.

item.title: In Mideast and worldwide, cautious relief over deal to end damaging war
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: Some world leaders hailed the deal, but many in the Middle East were cautious, saying important details remain to be settled and warning the accord could easily fall apart.

item.title: Britain announces sweeping ban on most social media for children under 16
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: The U.K. prohibition is set to go further than those of other governments that are lining up against platforms engineered to maximize the time children spend on them

item.title: Russian attack kills at least five and damages historic monastery in Kyiv
item.dc:creator: David Stern, Anastacia Galouchka
item.description: UNESCO, the U.N.’s world heritage agency, condemned the attack, which damaged the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery. Russia said it bombed military targets.

item.title: Trump sought to break Iran’s regime. He settled for reopening Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum
item.description: The emerging agreement ends a costly war but leaves Iran’s leadership intact and its nuclear future still subject to negotiation.

item.title: U.S. singer Oliver Tree among six killed in helicopter crash in Brazil
item.dc:creator: Terrence McCoy
item.description: The electro-pop singer attracted millions of fans across the world with hits such as "Life Goes On” and “Miss You.”

item.title: Amid years of war, these Israelis are still pushing for peace
item.dc:creator: Lior Soroka
item.description: As Israel’s longest period of sustained armed conflict drags on, the country’s diminished peace movement finds itself struggling to be heard on the sidelines.
